title: HDX validate report
description: The full validation report the `validate` verb emits (spec §10/§14, R4): the §14 MUST checklist executed over the discovery layer. Mirrors the validate-local DTO 1:1: exactly {checks, conformant}, additionalProperties:false at every level. `checks` lists EVERY §14 check id (the closed set of 20: M1–M6, L1–L3, I1–I3, H1–H2, T1–T2, G1–G3, Geo1), each recording whether it `ran` or was `skipped` (the §14-note requirement: the validator MUST CLEARLY REPORT WHICH CHECKS RAN). A check that ran carries a `pass`/`fail` result; a skipped check carries `result: null` and a non-null `detail` reason (an honest R3 deferral, never a silent pass). `conformant` is the overall verdict — true iff no check that RAN failed (fail-closed; a skip never flips it, spec §14). A violated MUST is carried here as a `result: fail` outcome with `conformant: false`, NEVER as a raised error. This shape is versioned IMPLICITLY by the manifest `format_version` ONLY (the spec §0 hard cut) — there is no separate schema-version field; a shape change requires a `format_version` bump.

checks.description: The per-check outcomes, one per §14 MUST id, in spec order (spec §14). Every id in the closed set appears exactly once — ran or honestly skipped.

conformant.description: The overall verdict: true iff no check that RAN failed (spec §14). A skipped check never flips it (fail-closed applies only to a violated MUST that ran).

check_outcome.title: HDX §14 check outcome
check_outcome.description: One §14 MUST check's recorded result — EXACTLY {id, status, result, depth, detail} (spec §14). additionalProperties:false forbids any derived/semantic key; the outcome is inert (id + ran/skip + pass/fail + R3 depth + opaque detail only). `result` is null iff the check was skipped; `detail` carries the fail or skip reason (null when none).

id.description: The stable spec check id (the closed set of the 20 §14 MUST ids).

status.description: Whether the check ran or was skipped (the §14-note ran/skip requirement).

result.description: The pass/fail verdict of a check that ran, or null for a skipped check.

depth.description: The R3 enforcement-depth class (architecture §7 R3): metadata_deep (metadata + 1-D index reads only) or byte_deep (would need a chunk/pixel/full-axis read).

detail.description: The opaque fail reason or skip reason, or null when none (not interpreted by HDX).
